title: Decoder PWL/logit sensitivity ladder
hypothesis: The broad-v2 shared PWL misses preserve top-k and are not caused by q8 reciprocal precision. A focused ladder over exact logit precision, unquantized PWL, PWL input/weight precision, and normalization precision should identify whether q8/bf16 precision is the immediate blocker or whether the PWL curve itself needs redesign.

direct_comparison.primary_question: On the two broad-v2 shared exact-token misses and nearby controls, which precision boundary first recovers exact next-token behavior while retaining top-k?
include: exact-softmax fp16/bf16 softmax input and weight variants
PWL q12/q10/q8/q6 input and weight variants
q8 PWL exact and reciprocal-normalization variants
exclude: claiming broad distribution robustness for any recovered row

expected_benefit: separates PWL curve error from precision and normalization error
identifies whether q12 or fp16-style PWL should be promoted to the next broad check
keeps exact next-token misses tied to concrete focus samples before broader reruns
risks: the focus set is intentionally small and cannot prove broad robustness
exact next-token behavior is highly margin-sensitive for the pinned tiny decoder
q12 or fp16-style recovery may raise hardware cost enough to change the frontier
